description:
## Parent

Parent epic: `bsm-dbh` — Filtering and search issue list views.

## Background

Beadsmith must remain a desktop client for Beadwork rather than a second issue tracker. The Issue Explorer currently loads a single issue collection through the existing `list_issues` path. The filtering/search epic requires the explorer to preload the three authoritative base collections that define the feature: All Issues, Ready, and Blocked.

All Issues is every Beadwork Issue in the Current Workspace across stored statuses and must come from `bw list --all --json`; `bw list --json` is not enough because it follows Beadwork's default actionable/open semantics. Ready and Blocked are workflow views, not fields that Beadsmith should derive locally. ADR-0005 says Beadsmith must call `bw ready --json` and `bw blocked --json` and preserve those commands' membership and ordering.

This task establishes the native/RPC/frontend data-loading contract that every later sidebar, search, empty-state, and e2e slice depends on.

## What to build

Build one combined Issue Explorer load path that returns the Current Workspace path plus three normalized Issue collections:

- `allIssues`, loaded from `bw list --all --json`.
- `readyIssues`, loaded from `bw ready --json`.
- `blockedIssues`, loaded from `bw blocked --json`.

The combined load should be all-or-nothing. If any required command fails, the Issue Explorer load fails as a whole using the existing issue-list error taxonomy instead of rendering partial data or inventing local fallback semantics.

## Technical Approach

Extend the Rust Beadwork issue adapter so the existing raw Issue parser can be reused for all three commands. Keep Beadwork CLI JSON as the only integration surface. Do not parse rendered TTY text or inspect Beadwork's git storage.

Preserve the existing normalized frontend `Issue` shape for each collection. `bw blocked --json` can include extra fields such as `open_blockers`; ignore those extras for this slice and keep dependency UI sourced from the normal Issue fields. If comments/docs currently say the raw parser only represents `bw list --all --json`, update them so it is clear the parser now supports Beadwork Issue JSON returned by list/ready/blocked commands.

Expose a new combined TauRPC command, `load_issue_explorer_data`, returning the workspace path and the three collections. Keep the existing `list_issues` command available unless a separate cleanup explicitly removes it and updates all tests/docs. Update generated TypeScript bindings and the frontend Effect service to consume the combined command for the Issue Explorer.

The frontend success state should hold all three collections even when any or all are empty. Retire the legacy idea that an empty issue array requires a separate load status for this path; true-empty UI decisions should later derive from `allIssues.length === 0`.

## Considerations

- Ready and Blocked must not be derived from `blockedBy`, status, parent, deferral, or other fields in React or Rust.
- Preserve output order from each Beadwork command. Do not sort or join by All Issues order.
- Treat `null`, empty stdout, and `[]` as successful empty command results where the existing parser already does so.
- Reuse the existing error categories: `missingBinary`, `notBeadworkWorkspace`, `commandFailed`, `parseFailed`, and `executionFailed`.
- It is acceptable for a Ready/Blocked failure to prevent All Issues from rendering in this slice; semantically wrong partial data is worse than an explicit failure.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] The Issue Explorer load path calls `bw list --all --json`, `bw ready --json`, and `bw blocked --json` behind one combined RPC/service operation.
- [ ] The combined response includes `workspacePath`, `allIssues`, `readyIssues`, and `blockedIssues`.
- [ ] Empty collections are represented as a successful loaded result rather than a separate legacy empty load state.
- [ ] Ready and Blocked parsing is null-tolerant and normalizes into the same frontend Issue contract as All Issues.
- [ ] Extra Blocked-only fields such as `open_blockers` are accepted/ignored and are not surfaced in UI contracts for this slice.
- [ ] If any required command fails, the combined load fails as a whole with the existing issue-list error taxonomy.
- [ ] The existing `list_issues` RPC remains available unless intentionally removed by a separate cleanup.

## Test Plan

- Rust adapter tests for the exact command invocations for All, Ready, and Blocked.
- Rust adapter/parser tests for `null`, `[]`, representative non-empty Ready output, and representative non-empty Blocked output containing `open_blockers`.
- RPC tests for successful combined payload mapping and for whole-load failure when any one underlying command fails.
- Type generation test asserting the new `load_issue_explorer_data` binding and combined response fields exist.
- Frontend Effect service tests for loading, success-with-empty-collections, and failure mapping.
title: Load Issue Explorer data through one authoritative All/Ready/Blocked RPC
